title: stevia setifera
intro: Stevia setifera is a perennial plant native to the deserts of North Africa and the Mediterranean region, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a close relative of Stevia rebaudiana, which is widely cultivated for its sweet-tasting leaves. Stevia setifera contains a range of medicinal compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves can be prepared as an herbal tea or dried for use in capsules, providing health benefits such as reduced blood pressure, improved digestion, and antioxidant effects.
